title: Smithery Agent/Server Submission Process Research
method: Web form (primary) + CLI (advanced) + REST API (for programmatic)

url_method.description: Bring your own hosting - enter your server's public HTTPS URL
requirements: Server will be scanned automatically to extract metadata (tools, prompts, resources)
If scan fails, provide a static server card at /.well-known/mcp/server-card.json

local_mcpb_method.description: For local stdio servers - publish pre-built MCPB bundle

requirements: Supports hosted (JS module upload), external (URL), and stdio (MCPB bundle) release types

special_instructions.server_creation_first: Before publishing, you may need to create a server via PUT /servers/{namespace}/{server} (idempotent)
special_instructions.scanning_process: Smithery scans your server to extract metadata. For public servers scan is automatic; for auth-required servers you'll be prompted to authenticate.
special_instructions.static_server_card: If automatic scanning cannot complete, provide metadata at /.well-known/mcp/server-card.json with fields: serverInfo (required), authentication (optional), tools, resources, prompts (optional)
special_instructions.verification: After publishing, visit Settings → Verification page to complete official-vendor verification checklist
special_instructions.user_agent_whitelist: SmitheryBot/1.0 (+https://smithery.ai) — whitelist this if your server has WAF/bot protection
special_instructions.namespace_requirement: Servers are organized under namespaces (e.g., @org/server-name). Create a namespace first if you don't have one.
special_instructions.config_schema: For session configuration, provide JSON Schema with x-from extension (see docs/build/session-config)

actionable_steps: 1. Go to https://smithery.ai/new to start the web form submission
2. Enter your MCP server's public HTTPS URL (Streamable HTTP transport required)
3. Smithery will scan your server to extract metadata automatically
4. If your server requires auth, authenticate during the scan
5. Optionally serve a static server card at /.well-known/mcp/server-card.json for manual metadata
6. Complete the publishing flow in the UI
7. After publishing, go to Settings → Verification to get verified
8. Alternative: Use CLI (smithery mcp publish) or REST API for programmatic publishing

registry_api_note.status: The registry.smithery.ai root returns 404, but the API endpoints work with proper paths
